input.text:
SHIFT HANDOFF REPORT

Bed 1: Mrs. Patricia Reyes (P1), 84, s/p ORIF day 4. Allergies: penicillin. Full code. Vitals stable (HR 78, RR 16, SpO2 97%, BP 132, Temp 36.8°C, NEWS2: 0). No acute change; alert and oriented. Enoxaparin 40 mg SC given, acetaminophen PRN not required this shift. Ambulates with assist. 

Bed 2: Mrs. Elena Aquino (P2) — FOCAL PATIENT. 78, post-pneumonia day 6, CHF and T2DM (on furosemide, metformin). DNR; POLST on file. Allergies: sulfa. Vitals: HR 88, RR 17, SpO2 95%, BP 124, Temp 37.1°C, NEWS2: 1. Noted to be more pale and withdrawn than previous days. Poor appetite — picking at breakfast. Vitals have not acutely changed but behaviorally appears at risk for decline or subtle infection recurrence. Given azithromycin 250 mg (final dose), furosemide 20 mg, metformin 500 mg as scheduled. RECOMMEND close monitoring for further subtle changes — consider checking again early.

Bed 3: Mr. Walter Goldberg (P3), 91, dementia with recurrent UTI. DNR/DNI/DNH; POLST. No known allergies. Vitals: HR 92, RR 20, SpO2 93%, BP 110, Temp 37.6°C, NEWS2: 3. Pleasant, no acute change from baseline, oriented x1. Ceftriaxone 1 g IV given as scheduled.

General: No acute issues on rounds, but recommend extra attention to Mrs. Aquino for any new behavioral, cognitive, or subtle physical changes. All scheduled meds administered. Call provider early if Mrs. Aquino deteriorates or shows increased confusion, agitation, or hypoxia.

tool_calls.output_text: Handoff recorded. Score: 0.82 You are now RN2. Call read_handoff() to begin shift2.
